requiresAutoCalendarPeriod.description: Used for period-specific analyses to indicate the defined or available calendar period must be of type autoCalendar

requiresAvailableAnalysisPeriod.description: Used for period-specific analyses to indicate the temporal dimension must be associated with one or more analysis periods

requiresDefinedAnalysisPeriod.description: Used for period-specific analyses to indicate the measure must be associated with one or more analysis periods

supportsMasterItems.description: If analysis can work with master items (default is true)

isHidden.description: whether the field is hidden in business logic
whether the master item is hidden in business logic

isDefinedLogicalModelValid.description: set only if previous property is true, to indicate if the business logic passes validation

isLogicalModelEnabled.description: if the analysis model is constructed based on a user-defined business-logic (as opposed to a default one)

locale.description: Set custom locale instead of the system default

spaceId.description: The space ID of the application

subpath.description: Content library relative listing path. Empty in case of root listed or representing actual subpath listed.

ChartType.description: Chart type given to current recommendation

Classifications.description: classification defines the default role that attribute can play in an analysis

CompositionMinMax.description: Upper and lower bounds for items of specific classification types

has_section_access.description: If set to true, the app has section access configured.

static_byte_size.description: Static memory usage for the app.

tables_profiling_data.description: Profiling data of the tables in the app.

EngineSize.description: Engine size override for app placement on non reload engines.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.

meta.description: Additional properties relating to the error.

source.description: References to the source of the error.

parameter.description: The URI query parameter that caused the error.

pointer.description: A JSON Pointer to the property that caused the error.

FieldAttributes.description:
Sets the formatting of a field. The properties of _qFieldAttributes_ and the formatting mechanism are described below. ### Formatting mechanism The formatting mechanism depends on the type set in _qType,_ as shown below:
In case of inconsistencies between the type and the format pattern, the format pattern takes precedence over the type.
### Type is DATE, TIME, TIMESTAMP or INTERVAL The following applies: * If a format pattern is defined in _qFmt_ , the formatting is as defined in _qFmt_ . * If _qFmt_ is empty, the formatting is defined by the number interpretation variables included at the top of the script ( _TimeFormat_ , _DateFormat_ , _TimeStampFormat_ ). * The properties _qDec_ , _qThou_ , _qnDec_ , _qUseThou_ are not used. ### Type is INTEGER The following applies: * If a format pattern is defined in _qFmt_ , the engine looks at the values set in _qDec_ and _qThou_ . If these properties are not defined, the formatting mechanism uses the number interpretation variables included at the top of the script ( _DecimalSep_ and _ThousandSep_ ). * If no format pattern is defined in _qFmt_ , no formatting is applied. The properties _qDec_ , _qThou_ , _qnDec_ , _qUseThou_ and the number interpretation variables defined in the script are not used . ### Type is REAL The following applies: * If a format pattern is defined in _qFmt_ , the engine looks at the values set in _qDec_ and _qThou_ . If these properties are not defined, the engine uses the number interpretation variables included at the top of the script ( _DecimalSep_ and _ThousandSep_ ). * If no format pattern is defined in _qFmt_ , and if the value is almost an integer value (for example, 14,000012), the value is formatted as an integer. The properties _qDec_ , _qThou_ , _qnDec_ , _qUseThou_ are not used. * If no format pattern is defined in _qFmt_ , and if _qnDec_ is defined and not 0, the property _qDec_ is used. If _qDec_ is not defined, the variable _DecimalSep_ defined at the top of the script is used. * If no format pattern is defined in _qFmt_ , and if _qnDec_ is 0, the number of decimals is 14 and the property _qDec_ is used. If _qDec_ is not defined, the variable _DecimalSep_ defined at the top of the script is used. ### Type is FIX The following applies: * If a format pattern is defined in _qFmt_ , the engine looks at the values set in _qDec_ and _qThou_ . If these properties are not defined, the engine uses the number interpretation variables included at the top of the script ( _DecimalSep_ and _ThousandSep_ ). * If no format pattern is defined in _qFmt_ , the properties _qDec_ and _qnDec_ are used. If _qDec_ is not defined, the variable _DecimalSep_ defined at the top of the script is used. ### Type is MONEY The following applies: * If a format pattern is defined in _qFmt_ , the engine looks at the values set in _qDec_ and _qThou_ . If these properties are not defined, the engine uses the number interpretation variables included at the top of any script ( _MoneyDecimalSep_ and _MoneyThousandSep_ ). * If no format pattern is defined in _qFmt_ , the engine uses the number interpretation variables included at the top of the script ( _MoneyDecimalSep_ and _MoneyThousandSep_ ). ### Type is ASCII No formatting, _qFmt_ is ignored.

Fmt.description:
Defines the format pattern that applies to _qText_ .
Is used in connection to the type of the field (parameter **qType** ).
For more information, see _Formatting mechanism_.
Example: _YYYY-MM-DD_ for a date.

Thou.description:
Defines the thousand separator (if any).
Is used if **qUseThou** is set to 1.
Example: **,**

UseThou.description:
Defines whether or not a thousands separator must be used.
Default is 0.

Average.description: Average of all numerical values. NaN otherwise.

AvgStringLen.description: Average string length of textual values. 0 otherwise.

DataEvenness.description: Data evenness aka Shannon's entropy normalized to [0,1]

FieldTags.description: List of tags related to the field.

FirstSorted.description: For textual values the first sorted string.

Fractiles.description: The .01, .05, .1, .25, .5, .75, .9, .95, .99 fractiles. Array of NaN otherwise.

Kurtosis.description: Kurtosis of the numerical values. NaN otherwise.

LastSorted.description: For textual values the last sorted string.

Max.description: Maximum value of numerical values. NaN otherwise.

MaxStringLen.description: Maximum string length of textual values. 0 otherwise.

Median.description: Median of all numerical values. NaN otherwise.

Min.description: Minimum value of numerical values. NaN otherwise.

MinStringLen.description: Minimum string length of textual values. 0 otherwise.

MostFrequent.description: Three most frequent values and their frequencies

Skewness.description: Skewness of the numerical values. NaN otherwise.

Std.description: Standard deviation of numerical values. NaN otherwise.

Sum.description: Sum of all numerical values. NaN otherwise.

Sum2.description: Squared sum of all numerical values. NaN otherwise.

SumStringLen.description: Sum of all characters in strings in the field

ZeroValues.description: Number of zero values for numerical values

always_one_selected.description:
If set to true, the field has one and only one selection (not 0 and not more than 1).
If this property is set to true, the field cannot be cleared anymore and no more selections can be performed in that field.
The default value is false.

byte_size.description: Static RAM memory used in bytes.

distinct_only.description:
If set to true, only distinct field values are shown.
The default value is false.

hash.description: Hash of the data in the field. If the data in a reload is the same, the hash will be consistent.

is_hidden.description:
If set to true, the field is hidden.
The default value is false.

is_locked.description:
If set to true, the field is locked.
The default value is false.

is_numeric.description:
Is set to true if the value is a numeric.
The default value is false.

is_semantic.description:
If set to true, the field is semantic.
The default value is false.

is_system.description:
If set to true, the field is a system field.
The default value is false.

tags.description:
Gives information on a field. For example, it can return the type of the field.
Examples: key, text, ASCII.

ownerId.description: The user that owns the filter, if missing the same as the request user.

FilterError.description: Error occured during the Filter creation.

code.description:
The unique code for the error

- "REP-400000" Bad request. The server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax.
- "REP-400008" Selections error.
- "REP-400015" Bad request in enigma request. The patch value has invalid JSON format.
- "REP-401000" Unauthorized. The client must authenticate itself to get the requested response.
- "REP-401001" Unauthorized, bad JWT.
- "REP-403000" Forbidden. The client does not have access rights to the content.
- "REP-403001" App forbidden, the user does not have read permission on the app.
- "REP-403002" Chart type not supported.
- "REP-404000" Not found. The server can not find the requested resource.
- "REP-409043" Filter name conflict. The filter name must be unique.
- "REP-429000" Too many request. The user has sent too many requests in a given amount of time ("rate limiting").
- "REP-429012" Exceeded max session tenant quota. A tenant has opened too many different sessions at the same time.
- "REP-429016" Exceeded max session tenant quota per day.
- "REP-500000" Fail to resolve resource.
- "REP-503005" Engine unavailable, qix-sessions error no engines available.
- "REP-503013" Session unavailable. The engine session used to create the report is unavailable.
- "REP-504042" Context deadline exceeded applying selections of the Filter.
- "REP-500031" Error creating bookmark.
- "REP-404032" Bookmark not found after creating the bookmark.
- "REP-500033" Error destroying bookmark.
- "REP-404033" Bookmark not found destroying the bookmark.
- "REP-409043" Dupliacate bookmark name.
- "REP-429034" Filters quota exceeded.
- "REP-400044" Missing or renamed field.
- "REP-403049" Report filter access not allowed.

title.description: A summary in english explaining what went wrong.
title.example: Bad request. The server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax.

FilterErrors.description: Errors occured during the Filter creation.

ownerId.description: The user that owns the filter, if missing the same as the request user.

data.description: a list of filters containing all the filters properties (like name,description...) except the filter definition (like FilterV1_0)

ownerId.description: The user that owns the filter, if missing the same as the request user.
The user that owns the filter, if missing the same as the request user.

fieldsByState.description: Map of fields to apply by state. Maximum number of states allowed is 125. Maximum number of fields allowed is 125 and maximum number of overall field values allowed is 150000.

JsonObject.description: Contains dynamic JSON data specified by the client.

cpu_time_spent_ms.description: Number of CPU milliseconds it took to reload the app.

fullReloadPeakMemoryBytes.description: Maximum number of bytes used during full reload of the app.

partialReloadPeakMemoryBytes.description: Maximum number of bytes used during partial reload of the app.

peak_memory_bytes.description: Maximum number of bytes used during reload of the app.

discriminator.description:
A string indicating the origin of the data:
* [filename]: the data comes from a local file.
* INLINE: the data is entered inline in the load script.
* RESIDENT: the data comes from a resident table. The table name is listed.
* AUTOGENERATE: the data is generated from the load script (no external table of data source).
* Provider: the data comes from a data connection. The connector source name is listed.
* [webfile]: the data comes from a web-based file.
* STORE: path to QVD or TXT file where data is stored.
* EXTENSION: the data comes from a Server Side Extension (SSE).

statement.description: The LOAD and SELECT script statements from the data load script.

log.description: Provides a link to download the log file.

create.description: Object create privileges. Hints to the client what type of objects the user is allowed to create.

privileges.description:
Application privileges.
Hints to the client what actions the user is allowed to perform.
Could be any of:
* read
* create
* update
* delete
* reload
* import
* publish
* duplicate
* export
* exportdata
* change_owner
* change_space

canCreate.description: Is set to true if the user has privileges to create the resource.

resource.description: Type of resource. For example, sheet, story, bookmark, etc.

NxAppObject.description: Application object attributes and user privileges.

Application object privileges.
Hints to the client what actions the user is allowed to perform.
Could be any of:
* read
* create
* update
* delete
* publish
* exportdata
* change_owner

NxAttributes.description: App attributes. This structure can also contain extra user-defined attributes.

createdDate.description: The date and time when the app was created.

dynamicColor.description: The dynamic color of the app.

encrypted.description: If set to true, the app is encrypted.

hasSectionAccess.description: If set to true, the app has section access configured,

isDirectQueryMode.description: True if the app is a Direct Query app, false if not

lastReloadTime.description: Date and time of the last reload of the app.

modifiedDate.description: The date and time when the app was modified.

originAppId.description: The Origin App ID for published apps.

owner.description: Deprecated. Use the Users API to fetch user metadata.

publishTime.description: The date and time when the app was published, empty if unpublished. Use to determine if an app is published in Qlik Cloud.

published.description:
For Qlik Cloud, indicates whether the app has been distributed from client-managed, not whether it has been published. To determine
if the app is published in Qlik Cloud, check for a non-empty value in the publishTime field. For client-managed, determines if the app is published.

NxObjectAttributes.description: App object attributes. This structure can also contain extra user-defined attributes.

approved.description: True if the object is approved.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the object was created.

publishedAt.description: The date and time when the object was published, empty if unpublished.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the object was modified.

Path.description: Path to the property to add, remove or replace.

Value.description:
This parameter is not used in a remove operation.
Corresponds to the value of the property to add or to the new value of the property to update.
Examples:
"false", "2", "\"New title\""

PartialNluInfo.description: Contains break down of the asked question in the form of tokens with their classification.

fieldName.description: Qlik sense application field selected for given token or phrase

role.description: Role of the token or phrase from query

text.description: Matching token or phrase from query

value.description: The value to be used for this operation. The properties that cannot be patched include id, filterType, appId

Placement.description: Override for app placement on non reload engines.

createdAt.description: The iso timestamp for when the override was configured.

moveApp.description: The original is moved instead of copied. The current published state of all objects is kept.

originAppId.description: If app is moved, originAppId needs to be provided.

spaceId.description: The managed space ID where the app will be published.

RecommendFieldItem.description: structure for providing fields in recommendation request, user can retrieve the fields using insight-analyses/model endpoint

id.description: id of the target analysis, returned by the GET insight-analyses endpoint

RecommendMasterItem.description: structure for providing master items in recommendation request, user can retrieve the libId of master item using insight-analyses/model endpoint

relevance.description: percentage of selected items in the analysis to the overall items passed to the endpoint

accessDbHash.description: A Base64-encoded hash value of the new section access database.

appDbHash.description: A Base64-encoded hash value of the new app database.

includeFiles.description: Files brought into the script via include/mustInclude macros.

isPartialReload.description: True if the reload is a partial reload.

rowLimit.description: If greater than or equal 0, defines max number of rows loaded from a data source.

skipStore.description:
Set to true to skip Store statements.
The default value is false.

statements.description: List of external loaded or stored statements.

storeHash.description: A Base64-encoded hash value of all fields stored via the store statements.

success.description: true if the reload was successful.

label.description: Label of the resource level node in lineage.

partialReloadOperation.description: Partial load operation. e.g. add/replace/update/merge. n/a when not in partial load mode.

tableName.description: Name of the source table in lineage.

checkOriginAppId.description: Validate that source app is same as originally published.

targetId.description: The target ID to be republished.

Column.description: UTF-8 byte column of the issue.

Info.description: Additional information like workarounds or clarifications.

Line.description: Line in the tab of the issue.

Msg.description: Error or warning string. What is the problem.

Tab.description: The index of the tab for the issue.

success.description: True if the reload was successful.

Frequency.description: Frequency of the above symbol in the field

Number.description: Numeric value of the symbol. NaN otherwise.

Text.description: String value of the symbol. This parameter is optional and present only if Symbol is a string.

byte_size.description: Static RAM memory used in bytes.

is_loose.description:
If set to true, the table is loose due to circular connection.
The default value is false.

is_semantic.description:
If set to true, the table is semantic.
The default value is false.

is_system.description:
If set to true, the table is a system table.
The default value is false.

NoOfRows.description: Number of rows in the table.

delete.description:
Deletes a media content file or complete directory.
Returns OK if the bytes containing the media file (or the complete content of a directory) were successfully deleted, or error in case of failure or lack of permission.

get.description:
Gets media content from file.
Returns a stream of bytes containing the media file content on success, or error if file is not found.

put.description:
Stores the media content file.
Returns OK if the bytes containing the media file content were successfully stored, or error in case of failure, lack of permission or file already exists on the supplied path.

get.description:
Lists media content.
Returns a JSON formatted array of strings describing the available media content or error if the optional path supplied is not found.

parameters.description: The path to sub folder with static content relative to the root folder. Use empty path to access the root folder.
parameters.name: path

parameters.description:
Optional. List output can include files and folders in different ways:
* Not recursive, default if show option is not supplied or incorrectly specified, results in output with files and empty directories for the path specified only.
* Recursive(r), use ?show=r or ?show=recursive, results in a recursive output with files, all empty folders are excluded.
* All(a), use ?show=a or ?show=all, results in a recursive output with files and empty directories.
parameters.name: show

get.description:
Gets media content from file currently used as application thumbnail.
Returns a stream of bytes containing the media file content on success, or error if file is not found.
The image selected as thumbnail is only updated when application is saved.

post.description:
Sets owner on an app object.
The user must be the owner of the object.

Reload metadata contains reload information, including reload id, duration, endtime and lineage load info. Data is available for the last 10 reloads of an application.

get.description: List all filters that are present in the given app. Filters allow to reduce the app data visible in a report output. Each filter can contain definitions on one or multiple fields.

parameters.description: The filter type (REP, SUB). REP stands for report bookmark, SUB for subscription bookmark.
parameters.name: filterTypes

parameters.description:
The advanced filtering to use for the query. Refer to [RFC 7644](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/rfc7644/) for the syntax. Cannot be combined with any of the fields marked as deprecated. All conditional statements within this query parameter are case insensitive.
The following fields support the `co` (contains) operator: `name`, `description`
The following fields support the `eq` (equals) operator: `ownerId`
Example:
(name co "query1" or description co "query2") and ownerId eq "123"
parameters.name: filter

parameters.description: Load type expressing the kind of request, eg. interactive for report requests from the Web UI, batch for scheduled report generation.
parameters.name: loadType

parameters.description: If present, the cursor that starts the page of data that is returned.
